meta_001: Personal Color Self-Check — Which season am I?
meta_002: One look in the mirror, 12 questions. Spring Warm · Summer Cool · Autumn Warm · Winter Cool — find the color your face loves.
html_004: One look in the mirror and you’re ready.
html_009: ① Get a mirror or a selfie ready in natural light (by a window).
html_010: ② A bare, makeup-free face and wrist give the truest read.
html_011: ③ If you’re torn, just pick the one that’s ‘a little closer’.
html_013:
※ This is a reference self-check based on self-observation. Results can shift with lighting or screen settings,
    so for an accurate read we recommend a professional draping test.
html_014: Two secret axes move in real time — what they measure is revealed at the end!
html_018: ✨ Colors that bring you to life
html_022: ※ This is a self-check for reference. Even within the same season, everyone’s finer tones differ.
html_023: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_001: In natural light, the veins on your inner wrist look…?
js_004: Gold vs silver jewelry — which lifts your face more?
js_007: If you wear white, which suits you better?
js_010: After a lot of sun, your skin…?
js_011: Tans to a nice golden brown
js_013: Which lip color gets you a ‘you look so alive’?
js_016: Which closet color gets the most ‘that looks great on you’?
js_021: Rose pink and lavender look radiant
js_022: Your eye color and the impression it gives?
js_028: When you wear deep colors like burgundy or dark navy…?
js_033: I look a little dull and washed out
js_034: What do people say when they first meet you?
js_038: The clear, radiant ‘spark fairy’ — bright warm tones switch on a light in your face.
js_051:
Styling — heavy, dark colors swallow your face. Keep tops bright; send dark shades down to your bottoms.
Makeup — coral lip + peach blush is the formula. A dewy finish beats matte on you.
Hair — light brown, or an orange-brown with no ashy tint.
js_053: The clear, cool ‘watercolor human’ — soft cool tones glow on top of pastels.
js_065:
Styling — soft, watered-down pastels beat vivid primaries. Grayed-off shades look especially good on you.
Makeup — rose-pink lip + lavender highlighter. Just avoiding orange tones gets you halfway there.
Hair — ash brown or ash purple; skip any dye that turns yellow.
js_067: The deep, mellow ‘mood master’ — rich earth tones pull up an air of luxury.
js_077:
Styling — every fallen-leaf color is yours. Layer low-saturation, warm-yellow deep shades and your whole mood levels up.
Makeup — MLBB brown lip + bronze shadow. Matte suits you more than shimmer.
Hair — chocolate brown or dark copper.
js_079: The vivid, striking ‘high-contrast lead’ — a rare type who can pull off bold colors.
js_086:
Styling — middling colors are the real poison. Win with black & white high contrast, or one single primary-color accent.
Makeup — a red or berry lip is your holy grail. A faint MLBB can make you look unwell.
Hair — blue-black or dark ash; skip bleaching to light brown.

_app.title: Personal Color Self-Check
_app.desc: One look in the mirror, 12 questions — find the season your face lives in.
